response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior full-stack engineer with genuine systems experience (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby), but he falls far short of the role's hard requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ programming; his professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project a decade ago. His large-scale infrastructure experience totals about two years rather than five, his cloud work is application-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) rather than platform architecture, and he has never held a formal technical-leadership role setting strategy for a compute platform. The profile is a web/product craftsman, not a C++ infrastructure lead.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus open-source authorship of test frameworks and libraries
Demonstrated mentoring inclination through workshops, refactoring dojos, and multi-day courses
concerns: C++ experience (~7 months professional, in 2014-2015) is drastically below the required 8 years; primary systems language is DLang
Only ~2 years of large-scale distributed-systems work against a 5-year requirement, and no production cloud-platform architecture ownership
No formal technical-lead experience owning cross-team strategy, priorities, and deliverables for an internal platform
